---
title: "Purpose and Reading Guide"
---

## What this book promises

This book explains the implementation that exists in this repository. It is
not a generic Swift tutorial and it does not describe an imaginary future
architecture. After reading it, a C programmer who already understands
transformers should be able to:

1. rebuild the application skeleton;
2. capture and resample microphone audio on macOS;
3. embed the selected `voxtral.c` runtime behind a stable C ABI;
4. understand every functional region of the vendored `voxtral.c`;
5. connect C ownership to Swift concurrency without racing the stream;
6. build the caption state machine and non-activating overlay;
7. run Gemma behind a loopback-only OpenAI-compatible service;
8. validate model output before replacing accessibility text;
9. implement authenticated local HTTP, Server-Sent Events, and anonymous
   question submission;
10. generate and reason about the QR codes used for pairing;
11. reproduce the build, tests, packaging, and security boundaries.

The source is the final authority. Paths and line references in this book refer
to the checked-out revision from which the book was rendered.

## Intended reader

The explanations assume:

- fluency in C, pointers, ownership, threads, and sockets;
- familiarity with transformer attention, KV caches, tokenizers, and
  autoregressive decoding;
- no prior Swift, SwiftUI, AppKit, Network.framework, or Core Image knowledge.

Swift is therefore introduced by comparing it with C concepts. The text avoids
teaching elementary transformer theory, but it explains the audio-specific and
streaming choices that make Voxtral different from an ordinary decoder-only
language model.

## Repository map

| Path | Responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| `Sources/ClassroomCaptionsCore` | Platform-neutral session, timeline, validation, and service contracts |
| `Sources/ClassroomCaptions` | macOS UI, audio capture, model orchestration, overlay, archive, and network server |
| `Sources/CVoxtralEngine/ClassroomVoxtral.c` | Stable project-owned C ABI |
| `Sources/CVoxtralEngine/Engine` | Pinned runtime derived from antirez's `voxtral.c` |
| `Sources/VoxtralBenchmark` | Offline speed and quality harness |
| `Tests/ClassroomCaptionsCoreTests` | Domain, bridge, command, export, and server tests |
| `Vendor/VoxtralEngine` | Upstream revision and license |
| `Documentation` | Decision records, measurements, and focused design notes |
| `docs` | This Quarto book |

## The central design rule

The accessibility path never waits for the language model:

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    subgraph Live["Immediate accessibility path"]
      direction LR
      Mic[Microphone PCM] --> Vox[Voxtral]
      Vox -->|provisional| Overlay[Overlay]
      Vox -->|finalized| Timeline[Immutable timeline]
      Timeline --> Overlay
    end
    subgraph Polish["Asynchronous optional correction"]
      direction LR
      Queue[Serialized queue] --> Gemma[Gemma]
      Gemma --> Validator[Deterministic validator]
      Validator -->|rejected| Raw[Keep raw caption]
    end
    Timeline --> Queue
    Validator -->|accepted| Timeline
```

Raw text is immutable. Correction is an optional field. A slow, unavailable, or
misbehaving Gemma process can reduce polish, but it cannot remove the live
Voxtral subtitle.

## How to read the book

Read @sec-system-tour first. C programmers should then read the Swift chapter
before following the audio and application coordinator code. The two Voxtral
chapters separate model architecture from source-level implementation.
Networking, QR, and security are deliberately separate because pairing is not
equivalent to encryption.

The final chapter gives a clean-room reconstruction order and verification
checklist.

## Build the book

From the repository root:

```bash
./scripts/build_book.sh
```

The script first regenerates the complete `voxtral.c` source chapter and
verifies that its code blocks reproduce all 1,652 source lines. It then renders
HTML and PDF with Quarto. Outputs are written under `docs/_build`.

To render one format while editing prose:

```bash
python3 scripts/generate_voxtral_source_chapter.py
quarto render docs --to html
quarto render docs --to typst
```
